title: Planet Labs Quarterly Results - Consistent Revenue Growth and Margin Expansion
text:
Planet Labs investor relations website provides detailed quarterly results demonstrating consistent revenue growth and operational improvement across multiple periods. The company's fiscal 2026 results show revenue of $307.7 million with 26% year-over-year growth and achievement of first full year non-GAAP profitability with $15.5 million adjusted EBITDA.

Quarterly results demonstrate acceleration in revenue growth, with Q4 reaching $86.8 million (41% YoY growth) and Q3 reaching $81.3 million (33% YoY growth). This acceleration reflects strong demand in defense and government segments. The company's ability to consistently beat analyst expectations (Q4 beat by 11.55%) validates the strength of its market position.

Backlog metrics show exceptional growth with the company's backlog reaching $900 million (79% YoY growth) and remaining performance obligations growing 106% to $852 million. These metrics provide visibility for future revenue generation. The company's high contract value from recurring annual and multi-year contracts (approximately 85% of revenue) supports revenue predictability.

Segment performance highlights include Defense & Intelligence revenue growing over 50% year-over-year, driven by government contracts and AI-enabled solutions. The company's focus on high-value government and defense customers provides pricing power and stability. Operating leverage improvements are evident in free cash flow performance, reaching $52.9 million in fiscal 2026 from negative $64 million previously.

Investor materials emphasize the company's position as a leader in Earth observation and geospatial intelligence. Strategic initiatives including the NVIDIA AI partnership, satellite constellation expansion to 32 spacecraft, and international contract expansion position the company for continued growth. Management guidance of $415-440 million fiscal 2027 revenue reflects confidence in sustained expansion.
